findings.title: Log command tails a filename workers do not write
findings.impact: Normal workers started through dscli use the configured log_dir and log_filename flags; the default config sets log_dir to ./datasystem/logs, worker startup normalizes it, and logging writes files such as datasystem_worker.INFO/WARNING/ERROR. This changed line instead hard-codes /opt/yuanrong/datasystem/log/<address>.log, so dscli logs -w <addr> fails with tail errors for the standard worker configuration and cannot print the requested log.
findings.fix: Resolve the matching worker's actual log_dir and log_filename from its command line or config, then tail the existing severity log file pattern used by Logging, instead of deriving a fixed <address>.log path.
